← the Atlas

Remote terminals — the finale (R9–R10)

budding·proposed·JUN 28, 2026

The corrected R9–R10 plan. R8 shipped the right reader-join (keep it) but made kolu SERVE awareness in-process — re-coupling awareness into kolu, the exact thing pulam was built to decouple. The fix — kolu CONSUMES a pulam instead of serving awareness itself (a local pulam locally, a remote one remotely), so the arrow points to pulam with no cross-reach and the connectTerminalWorkspace mud never arises. R9.0 fixes the decoupling; collapsing to literally one pulam per host (pulam-web reading kolu's pulam too) is a separate optional phase, R9-converge. Phases — R9.0 decouple · R9.1 resolver (shipped) · R9.2 ssh PTY · R9.3 remote awareness · R9.4 reconnect · R9.5 Code-tab fs/git · R9-converge (optional) · R10 canvas.

Contents

  • Roadmap
  • What R9 reuses — and what it must build
  • The sub-phases
    • R9.0 — kolu consumes a local pulam (retire in-process awareness) · the decouple
    • R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped
    • R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile
    • R9.3 — remote awareness = the same mirror, a remote pulam
    • R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption
    • R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)
    • R9-converge — one pulam per host (optional, deferred)
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • Open decisions

The last leg of #951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8 (#1594) shipped the right reader-join (a terminal is kolu.authored ⋈ terminalWorkspace.awareness, joined at the reader) — keep that. But R8 also made kolu serve awareness in-process (the registry-projection backing of serveTerminalWorkspace), which re-coupled awareness into kolu — the exact thing pulam (R4) exists to decouple. This note corrects course: kolu consumes a pulam instead of serving awareness itself — a supervised local pulam for the local box (R9.0), a remote pulam for a remote host (R9.3, the same mirror). The arrow points → pulam and neither side reaches into the other, so the connectTerminalWorkspace mud never arises. (Collapsing to literally one pulam per host — pulam-web reading kolu’s pulam too — is a separate, optional step: R9-converge.) The parent remote-terminals keeps the one-line rows; full model in the terminal model.

The correction — why R9a is discarded

R8’s “kolu serves its own awareness” left awareness with two shapes (kolu multiplexed vs pulam standalone) and no single authority. That forced R9a (#1604) to teach pulam-web how to reach into kolu (a kolu-specific connectTerminalWorkspace slice) — composing an intermediary, not pulam. The root fix is upstream: kolu consumes a pulam instead of serving awareness itself, so neither side reaches into the other and the mud is gone. So R9a is abandoned — its approach (pulam-web reaching into kolu) is exactly the mud we’re removing; its narrow localhost desync is split out to the optional R9-converge. The one real thing it surfaced — a @kolu/surface membership bug (broadcastKeys) — lands in its own PR.

The backing is ALWAYS a pulam mirror — local or remote

R8’s reader-join and the serveTerminalWorkspace awareness-backing injection seam are kept. Only the backing changes: instead of kolu’s in-process sensors, kolu’s awareness backing is a mirror of a pulam — a local pulam for the local box (R9.0), a remote pulam for a remote host (R9.3), through the same mirror code path. The arrow always points → pulam — kolu never serves awareness itself. (Bonus: the local pulam owns the kaval byte-tap, so the old “kolu has no live activity” gap closes for free.)

Roadmap

FINALE — R9 (corrected) · R10✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓R9.0 · kolu consumes a local pulam (retire in-process awareness)the decouple · #1614
✓R9.1 · the TerminalEndpoint resolver (orthogonal — shipped)#1603
○R9.2 · ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tileneeds R9.1
○R9.3 · remote awareness = the SAME mirror, a remote pulamneeds R9.0 · R9.2 · R7
○R9.4 · persisted location + reconnect + adoptionneeds R9.2
○R9.5 · Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulseneeds R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3
◇R9-converge · (OPTIONAL, deferred) pulam-web reads kolu's pulam → one pulam/hostoff critical path · needs a transport generalization
○R10 · canvas multiplex + validateneeds R9.5

R9.0 is the new foundation — it retires R8’s in-process backing and removes R9a’s mud (the narrow localhost single-instance desync is split out to the optional R9-converge). R9.1 shipped (it’s the PTY-endpoint path, orthogonal to awareness — unaffected by the correction). Only R9.5 is gated on R-pulamweb-4.

What R9 reuses — and what it must build

Shipped seams (reuse as-is):

seam where leg it serves
serveTerminalWorkspace @kolu/terminal-workspace the awareness-backing injection point — now fed by a pulam mirror, not in-process sensors
the pulam daemon @kolu/pulam (R4) the one sensor set per host; serves terminalWorkspaceSurface standalone
the daemon-supervisor spine @kolu/surface-daemon-supervisor (R2) adoptOrEnsure to bring up / adopt a local pulam, the same way it manages the kaval
frontDaemonOverStdio @kolu/surface-daemon dial a remote kaval over ssh stdio → the remote PTY
getHostSession @kolu/surface-nix-host a pooled, long-lived session to dial a remote pulam → the mirror
mirrorRemoteSurface @kolu/surface the total dual ({ procedures, done }, R7) — mirror a pulam’s surface into one local handle
HostLocation remote arm · persisted location @kolu/common { kind:"remote", hostId }, round-trips to disk

Must build:

gap today the phase
retire kolu’s in-process awareness sensors; back kolu’s awareness with a local-pulam mirror R8 runs sensors in-process (registry-projection backing) R9.0
the ssh driver (the remote PTY arm) only {kind:"local"} exists R9.2
point the same mirror at a remote pulam — R9.3 (trivial once R9.0 lands)

The sub-phases

R9.0 — kolu consumes a local pulam (retire in-process awareness) · the decouple #1614

R9.0· kolu consumes a local pulam; pulam is the awareness authority✓ shipped
needs ← R8 · R4links #1614

The fault R8 left. R8 made kolu run the awareness sensors in-process and serve them (the registry-projection backing). That re-coupled awareness into kolu and gave awareness two serving shapes (kolu multiplexed, pulam standalone) — the asymmetry R9a then papered over with a kolu-aware mirror.

The fix. Run one local pulam — it dials kolu’s own kaval, runs the one sensor set, and serves terminalWorkspaceSurface standalone. kolu’s awareness backing becomes a mirror of that local pulam, injected at the same serveTerminalWorkspace seam R9.3 uses for remote. kolu’s Dock keeps the reader-join unchanged. pulam-web keeps its own pulam (its existing ssh dial) — it does not reach into kolu, so the connectTerminalWorkspace mud never arises and the arrow points → pulam for both sides.

This closes the mud — the architectural defect — by decoupling kolu, with neither side reaching into the other. It does not collapse the two pulam instances into one; that’s the optional R9-converge. The relaxed invariant for R9.0: kolu is decoupled, both kolu and pulam-web point at a pulam, with no cross-reach.

Lifecycle — kaval survives, pulam is ephemeral

kaval owns the PTYs, so it must survive a kolu restart/redeploy: it’s a detached daemon kolu re-adopts on boot (R2) — terminals untouched. pulam owns nothing irreplaceable (its awareness is computed from the kaval), so it is ephemeral — it dies with kolu, and a fresh one re-derives all awareness from the surviving kaval on boot. The only cost is a brief awareness gap during the restart; no work is lost. So adoptOrEnsure for the local pulam effectively always ensures (spawns fresh), never adopts.

  • Decided — supervision: kolu-server owns the local pulam’s lifecycle via the R2 adoptOrEnsure spine (ephemeral, per the callout above) — boot brings up kaval → local pulam → mirror.
  • Decided — migration: a hard cutover — one PR deletes kolu’s in-process sensors and points the awareness backing at the local-pulam mirror, with no flag and no fallback (the no-knobs rule; the worst case on the local awareness path is a briefly-stale badge, never lost work).
  • Needs: R8 (the reader-join + the injection seam) and R4 (the pulam daemon) — both shipped.
  • Done: kolu-server runs no in-process awareness sensors; its backing is a mirror of its supervised local pulam; buildReServe is lifted to a shared home (both kolu and pulam-web consume it); the arrow points → pulam with no cross-reach (no connectTerminalWorkspace). Out of scope (→ R9-converge): collapsing to one pulam instance / killing the localhost Dock-vs-pulam-web desync.

R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver — shipped #1603

R9.1· HostLocation-keyed endpoint resolver✓ shipped
needs ← R8links #1603

The PTY-endpoint path — orthogonal to awareness, so the correction above doesn’t touch it. resolveTerminalEndpoint is the one HostLocation-keyed seam mapping a terminal to its kaval endpoint; the {kind:"remote"} arm fails loudly until R9.2 fills it (.exhaustive() forces a deliberate arm). Per-terminal ops resolve off entry.meta.location, so R9.2 is a one-case addition.

R9.2 — ssh kaval driver → a remote PTY tile

R9.2· ssh kaval driver — remote PTY○ todo
needs ← R9.1

Add the {kind:"remote",hostId} arm to the resolver: dial the remote host’s kaval over frontDaemonOverStdio (the same ssh <host> kaval --stdio relay kaval-tui --host uses) → a remote PTY on the canvas, PTY only (no awareness badge yet). Persist location = {kind:"remote",hostId}. Adoption reuses the local boot’s adoptOrEnsure spine + the pid-gate’s atomic link(2) (a dial joins a host’s kaval, never races to kill it).

  • Needs: R9.1.
  • Done: dial a host, spawn a PTY, see an ssh-badged remote tile that echoes; it survives a network blip via re-attach. e2e: a remote PTY round-trips over ssh.

R9.3 — remote awareness = the same mirror, a remote pulam

R9.3· remote awareness — the same mirror, remote source○ todo
needs ← R9.0 · R9.2 · R7

Because R9.0 already made kolu mirror a (local) pulam for its awareness backing, a remote host is the same backing pointed at the remote host’s pulam — dialed over a long-lived getHostSession, mirrored via R7’s total dual. Only the dial differs (a local pulam in-process vs a remote pulam over ssh); the backing-injection, the reader-join, and disk persistence are identical. The remote tile gains the git/agent/PR badge + green dot; activity rides the mirror.

  • Needs: R9.0 (the mirror backing exists), R9.2 (the remote PTY), R7 (the total dual).
  • Done: a remote tile shows live awareness mirrored from the remote pulam, via the same code path as local; a differential test pins it equal to the remote pulam’s own awareness.

R9.4 — persisted location, reconnect, adoption

R9.4· persisted location + reconnect + adoption○ todo
needs ← R9.2

Harden the remote lifecycle: re-validate the persisted location on reconnect, and cover the adoption/reconcile cases.

the remote kaval is… kolu does
absent provision + spawn fresh (adoptOrEnsure → ensure)
live · wire-compatible adopt — connect, never kill; its PTYs reconcile in
live · a build behind adopt + an “update pending” nudge
live · contract-skewed recycle (kill → respawn) — only on a typed DaemonContractSkewError
unreachable bounded retry, then degraded — never killed
  • Open (decide here): what is re-validated on reconnect and the fail behavior. location is the host discriminator (kolu.authored); cwd is the observed dir (terminalWorkspace.awareness) — different collections. Spell out the comparison and the fail-fast (crash loudly vs re-derive); don’t invent it at implementation time.
  • Needs: R9.2.
  • Done: a kolu-server restart re-adopts remote PTYs; each adoption-table row has a test.

R9.5 — the Code-tab fs/git rewrite (procedure + pulse)

R9.5· Code-tab fs/git → procedure + pulse○ todo
needs ← R-pulamweb-4 · R9.3

The one large client change, and the only finale phase R-pulamweb-4 gates. The Code tab reads koluSurface’s value-bearing streams today (CodeTab.tsx — app.streams.gitStatus/fsListAll/gitDiff.use). Rewrite it to the shared surface’s procedure + {seq} pulse: call the procedure once, re-query on each pulse (the two wire shapes are in surface live data). This deletes the last koluSurface fs/git reads and works for local and remote tiles alike.

  • Needs: R-pulamweb-4 (proves the procedure + pulse remote leg over websocketLink + surfaceClient), R9.3.
  • Done: the Code tab shows live git status/diff for a remote tile via procedure + pulse; no koluSurface fs/git reads remain.

R9-converge — one pulam per host (optional, deferred)

R9-converge· pulam-web reads kolu's local pulam — one pulam instance per host○ todo
needs ← R9.0

After R9.0 the arrow already points → pulam for both kolu and pulam-web, with no cross-reach — the mud is gone. What remains is two pulam instances on the local box (kolu’s supervised one + pulam-web’s own), which can drift: the narrow working-in-pulam-web vs idle-in-Dock desync. This phase collapses them to one by pointing pulam-web at kolu’s supervised local pulam.

Why it’s optional and off the critical path:

  • Narrow benefit. The desync only appears when pulam-web is deployed and pointed at localhost (a fleet dashboard watching its own box). The usual deployments — kolu alone locally, or pulam-web watching remote fleets — never hit it.

  • Chunky cost. pulam-web is ssh-locked: its host registry, HostSession, and pumpRemoteSurface assume ssh-stdio reconnect (“stdio links don’t recover mid-stream”). Dialing a local unix socket is not a config flip — it needs a transport generalization (a pluggable unix-socket | ssh session) inside @kolu/surface-nix-host.

  • Blocks nothing. R9.2–R9.5 and R10 don’t need it, and R9.3’s remote mirror reuses the existing ssh path — not this generalization. So it can land any time after R9.0, or never.

  • Needs: R9.0 (the local pulam exists) + the pulam-web transport generalization.

  • Done: under localhost, pulam-web reads kolu’s supervised local pulam (not its own); one pulam instance per host; the Dock-vs-pulam-web differential passes by construction (one source).

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + validate○ todo
needs ← R9.5

The UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. The granularity call lands here, validated, not up front.

  • Needs: R9 (all of it).
  • Done: the validated UX call, recorded.

Open decisions

Resolve each before the phase that needs it — so the implementer isn’t choosing mid-flight.

decision needed by options
local pulam lifecycle R9.0 decided — kolu-server adoptOrEnsures an ephemeral local pulam (R2 supervisor); it dies with kolu and re-derives on boot
migration off R8’s in-process backing R9.0 decided — hard cutover (no flag, no fallback)
rename the join TerminalMetadata → Terminal cosmetic, any time ride a mechanical pass (~63 refs), or leave it
reconnect re-validation semantics R9.4 what’s compared (location vs the remote kaval’s live cwd) + the fail-fast behavior

Referenced by

  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • The Terminal Model — two collections, one reader-side join (and what to name them)
